message:
feat: add visual stall indicators, agent naming, and controller command

- Pane activity indicators: green (active <30s), yellow (idle 30s-2min),
  red (stalled >2min) with configurable thresholds (bd-3v1w7)
- Agent naming with NATO phonetic alphabet: claude-alpha, codex-bravo,
  gemini-charlie. Custom names via --spawn-names flag (bd-1qls)
- ntm controller command: launches coordinator agent in pane 1 with
  default prompt and variable substitution. Robot mode support (bd-16291)
- 58 new tests across all three features

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-15yvf","title":"Task: Implement context pack caching layer","description":"# Task: Context Pack Caching

Cache generated context packs for reuse across ensemble runs.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/context_cache.go
type ContextCache struct {
    CacheDir string
    TTL      time.Duration
}

type CachedPack struct {
    Pack        *ContextPack
    Hash        string
    CachedAt    time.Time
    ExpiresAt   time.Time
    HitCount    int
}

func NewContextCache(cacheDir string, ttl time.Duration) *ContextCache
func (c *ContextCache) Get(projectRoot string) (*ContextPack, bool)
func (c *ContextCache) Put(projectRoot string, pack *ContextPack) error
func (c *ContextCache) Invalidate(projectRoot string) error
func (c *ContextCache) InvalidateAll() error
func (c *ContextCache) ComputeHash(pack *ContextPack) string
func (c *ContextCache) IsStale(pack *CachedPack) bool
```

## Cache Key
```
key = sha256(project_root + git_head_commit + problem_statement)
```

## Invalidation Triggers
- Git HEAD changes (new commits)
- Problem statement changes
- User explicit invalidation (--no-cache)
- TTL expiration (default 1 hour)

## Cache Location
```
~/.cache/ntm/context-packs/
├── <hash1>.json
├── <hash2>.json
└── metadata.json
```

## Logging
- Log cache hits/misses
- Log invalidation reasons
- Track hit rate in metrics

-{"id":"bd-16291","title":"Add ntm controller command to launch a dedicated controller agent in pane 1 with preset prompt","description":"Add ntm controller command to launch a dedicated controller agent in pane 1 with preset prompt.

## Problem Being Solved

Multi-agent swarms benefit from having a "controller" agent that coordinates the others. Currently this requires manual setup.

## Command Design

```bash
ntm controller SESSION [--prompt FILE] [--agent-type cc|cod|gmi]
```

### Behavior
1. Attaches to SESSION
2. Finds or creates pane 1
3. Launches agent with controller-specific prompt
4. Default prompt includes session overview and coordination instructions

### Default Controller Prompt
```
You are the controller agent for session {SESSION}.
Other agents in this session: {AGENT_LIST}
Your role: coordinate work, prevent conflicts, ensure quality.
Use ntm robot commands to monitor other agents.
```

### Custom Prompt
User can provide custom prompt file with variables:
- {SESSION}: session name
- {AGENT_LIST}: list of other agents
- {PROJECT}: project directory
- {BEADS}: open beads (if bv available)

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-controller-spawn=SESSION [--prompt=FILE]
Returns: JSON with controller pane info

## Acceptance Criteria

1. ntm controller command exists and shows in help
2. Command spawns agent in pane 1 of specified session
3. Default prompt includes session name and agent list
4. --prompt flag accepts path to custom prompt file
5. --agent-type flag allows choosing agent type (default: cc)
6. Robot mode variant returns JSON with pane ID

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/controller_test.go
- Test prompt variable substitution
- Test pane selection logic

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-CONTROLLER] Controller Command Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-CONTROLLER]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing ntm controller command"
TEST_SESSION="e2e_ctrl_$$"
ntm spawn "$TEST_SESSION" --cc=2 --no-prompt
sleep 5
# Test controller spawn
ntm controller "$TEST_SESSION" --no-prompt 2>&1 || echo "WARN: controller command not yet implemented"
ntm kill "$TEST_SESSION" --force
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Controller test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. ntm controller command launches dedicated agent in pane 1
2. Preset prompt includes project context
3. Agent has elevated permissions for coordination
4. Integration with agent mail for swarm control
5. Unit tests for command parsing
6. E2E test for controller lifecycle","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"CloudyGlen","created_at":"2026-01-20T05:27:38.891522309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:17.132978631Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-168bh","title":"Unit Tests: Watch mode loop and exit conditions","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for watch mode loop and exit conditions.

## Test Cases

### Watch Loop
- Initial assignment pass executes
- Completion detected triggers reassignment
- Unblocked beads detected and assigned
- Poll interval respected

### Exit Conditions
- Ctrl+C (SIGINT) triggers graceful shutdown
- --stop-when-done exits when no beads
- All agents crashed exits with error
- No more idle agents waits

### Streaming Output
- Timestamps accurate
- Assignment events logged
- Completion events logged
- Unblock events logged

### Error Handling
- BV unavailable mid-loop
- Agent Mail unavailable
- Assignment failure recovery

### Edge Cases
- Zero beads to start
- All beads blocked
- Single agent scenario
- Very fast completions

## Logging
- Log each loop iteration
- Log event processing
- Log exit reason

## Test File

@@ -232,7 +232,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1q93","title":"Fix progress bar rendering artifacts in context usage display","description":"## Problem
Context usage progress bar showing visual artifacts instead of clean bar.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Implementation Files:
- `internal/tui/components/progress.go:73-118` - ProgressBar.View()
- `internal/tui/styles/styles.go:199-252` - Shimmer() function
- `internal/tui/styles/styles.go:317-347` - ShimmerProgressBar()
- `internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:2606-2640` - renderContextBar()

### Characters Used:
- Filled: `█` (U+2588 Full Block)
- Empty: `░` (U+2591 Light Shade)
- Alternative: `━` `┄` in metrics panel

### How Shimmer Works:
```go
// Each character gets individual RGB color code
for i, r := range runes {
    pos := float64(i)/float64(n-1) + offset  // offset changes with tick
    // Calculate interpolated color
    color := Lerp(parsedColors[segmentIdx], parsedColors[segmentIdx+1], localPos)
    style := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color(fmt.Sprintf("#%02x%02x%02x", c.R, c.G, c.B)))
    result.WriteString(style.Render(string(r)))
}
```

### Potential Artifact Causes:
1. **True Color Fallback** - Terminal may not support 24-bit RGB, causing color dithering
2. **Per-character ANSI** - Each block has its own escape sequence (e.g., `\x1b[38;2;100;150;200m█\x1b[0m`)
3. **Font Glyph Width** - `█` and `░` may render at different widths in some fonts
4. **Animation Jitter** - Shimmer updates every 50ms causing visual noise
5. **Unicode Rendering** - Block characters may have combining issues

## Fix Approach

### 1. Add Terminal Capability Detection
```go
// internal/tui/terminal/caps.go
func SupportsTrueColor() bool {
    colorterm := os.Getenv("COLORTERM")
    return colorterm == "truecolor" || colorterm == "24bit"
}

func SupportsUnicodeBlocks() bool {
    // Check TERM and LC_CTYPE for Unicode support
    term := os.Getenv("TERM")
    return strings.Contains(term, "256color") || strings.Contains(term, "xterm")
}
```

### 2. Provide ASCII Fallback
```go
// Fallback characters for limited terminals
const (
    FilledASCII = "="
    EmptyASCII  = "-"
    FilledSimple = "#"
    EmptySimple  = "."
)

func (p ProgressBar) View() string {
    filled, empty := p.FilledChar, p.EmptyChar
    if !terminal.SupportsUnicodeBlocks() {
        filled, empty = FilledASCII, EmptyASCII
    }
    // ... rest of render
}
```

### 3. Reduce Shimmer Frequency
```go
// Change from 50ms to 100-200ms to reduce visual noise
func (p ProgressBar) tick() tea.Cmd {
    return tea.Tick(time.Millisecond*150, func(t time.Time) tea.Msg {
        return ProgressTickMsg(t)
    })
}
```

### 4. Simplify Color Gradient
```go
// Use solid color instead of per-character gradient for better stability
func SimpleProgressBar(percent float64, width int, filled, empty string, filledColor, emptyColor lipgloss.Color) string {
    filledWidth := int(percent * float64(width))
    emptyWidth := width - filledWidth
    
    filledStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(filledColor)
    emptyStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(emptyColor)
    
    return filledStyle.Render(strings.Repeat(filled, filledWidth)) +
           emptyStyle.Render(strings.Repeat(empty, emptyWidth))
}
```

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestProgressBarRendering(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name    string
        percent float64
        width   int
    }{
        {"empty", 0.0, 20},
        {"half", 0.5, 20},
        {"full", 1.0, 20},
        {"narrow", 0.5, 5},
        {"overflow", 1.5, 20},  // should clamp to 1.0
    }
    
    for _, tc := range tests {
        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            bar := NewProgressBar(tc.width)
            bar.SetPercent(tc.percent)
            bar.Animated = false  // Disable for deterministic test
            
            rendered := bar.View()
            
            // Verify display width matches expected
            expectedWidth := tc.width
            if bar.ShowPercent {
                expectedWidth += 5  // " 100%"
            }
            
            actualWidth := lipgloss.Width(rendered)
            if actualWidth != expectedWidth {
                t.Errorf("width = %d, want %d", actualWidth, expectedWidth)
            }
        })
    }
}

func TestShimmerStability(t *testing.T) {
    // Verify shimmer produces stable width across ticks
    text := strings.Repeat("█", 10)
    colors := []string{"#FF0000", "#00FF00", "#0000FF"}
    
    var widths []int
    for tick := 0; tick < 100; tick++ {
        result := styles.Shimmer(text, tick, colors...)
        widths = append(widths, lipgloss.Width(result))
    }
    
    // All widths should be identical (10)
    for i, w := range widths {
        if w != 10 {
            t.Errorf("tick %d: width = %d, want 10", i, w)
        }
    }
}

func TestASCIIFallback(t *testing.T) {
    bar := NewProgressBar(20)
    bar.SetPercent(0.5)
    bar.FilledChar = "="
    bar.EmptyChar = "-"
    bar.Animated = false
    
    rendered := bar.View()
    
    // Verify no Unicode block characters
    if strings.ContainsAny(rendered, "█░") {
        t.Error("ASCII fallback should not contain Unicode blocks")
    }
    
    // Verify correct character count
    stripped := stripANSI(rendered)
    equals := strings.Count(stripped, "=")
    dashes := strings.Count(stripped, "-")
    
    if equals != 10 || dashes != 10 {
        t.Errorf("got %d equals, %d dashes; want 10 each", equals, dashes)
    }
}
```

## E2E Test (VHS)
```tape

Output progress_bar_test.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard --demo-data"
Sleep 2s

Sleep 1s

# Verify clean rendering at different percentages
Screenshot progress_bar_screenshot.png

# Test resize behavior
Set Width 800
Sleep 1s
Screenshot progress_bar_narrow.png

Set Width 400
Sleep 1s
Screenshot progress_bar_very_narrow.png
```

## Verification Checklist
- [ ] Progress bar renders cleanly at 0%, 50%, 100%
- [ ] No artifacts during shimmer animation
- [ ] Width consistent across animation frames
- [ ] ASCII fallback works on limited terminals
- [ ] Works in tmux, screen, and native terminal
- [ ] No visual glitches during resize","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:08:44.578937798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:19:58.792722608Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:19:58.792593825Z","close_reason":"Implemented terminal capability detection (caps.go), ASCII fallbacks for progress bars, increased tick interval to 150ms. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q93","depends_on_id":"bd-1ay2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:15:52.126891994Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1q93","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:15:48.993314086Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1q93","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:36.013606643Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Surface a **progress summary** (beads completed vs assigned) in existing outputs rather than adding a new standalone progress command.

## Scope
- Add progress bar/ratio to `ntm status` and dashboard
- Add fields to `--robot-status` / `--robot-dashboard`
- Compute using assignment tracking + BV closed/ready counts

- E2E: assign beads → close beads → verify progress updates in status + robot output

`log.Printf("[E2E-PROGRESS] assigned=%d completed=%d ratio=%.2f", …)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Progress visible without new CLI entry
- Robot output includes progress fields
- Deterministic outputs for same state","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"SilentMarsh","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:40:31.268447007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T10:06:32.120069616Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T10:06:32.119920499Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qct","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:41Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1qfho","title":"Task: Implement ensemble dashboard keymap + shortcuts","description":"Implement keyboard shortcuts for ensemble dashboard navigation and synthesis control.

## Keyboard Shortcut Map

### Global Shortcuts
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| q | Quit dashboard |
| ? | Show help overlay |
| r | Refresh status |
| Esc | Close overlay/unfocus |

### Mode Navigation
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| j/↓ | Move to next mode |
| k/↑ | Move to previous mode |
| Enter | Zoom to selected window |
| Tab | Cycle focus between panels |

### Synthesis Control
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| s | Start synthesis (when ready) |
| S | Force synthesis (even if incomplete) |
| e | Export synthesis result |
| c | Copy synthesis to clipboard |

### View Controls
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| 1-9 | Jump to window N |
| [ | Decrease panel width |
| ] | Increase panel width |
| f | Toggle fullscreen mode panel |
| v | Toggle verbose mode info |

## Implementation
```go
// internal/tui/ensemble_keys.go
type EnsembleKeyMap struct {
    Quit           key.Binding
    Help           key.Binding
    Refresh        key.Binding
    NextMode       key.Binding
    PrevMode       key.Binding
    ZoomWindow     key.Binding
    CycleFocus     key.Binding
    StartSynthesis key.Binding
    ForceSynthesis key.Binding
    Export         key.Binding
    Copy           key.Binding
    JumpWindow     []key.Binding
}

func DefaultEnsembleKeyMap() EnsembleKeyMap
func (k EnsembleKeyMap) ShortHelp() []key.Binding
func (k EnsembleKeyMap) FullHelp() [][]key.Binding

// Key handler in main model
func (m EnsembleDashboard) handleKeyPress(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd)
```

### Help Overlay
Show formatted help when ? is pressed.

## File Location
internal/tui/ensemble_keys.go

## Dependencies
- Ensemble panel (bd-121pa)

## Logging Requirements
- Log key actions in debug mode","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T10:30:21.446232608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:56:12.614732938Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T19:56:12.614641227Z","close_reason":"EnsembleKeyMap struct, DefaultEnsembleKeyMap(), ShortHelp(), FullHelp(), JumpWindow bindings all implemented in ensemble_keys.go. Comprehensive tests in ensemble_keys_test.go.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qfho","depends_on_id":"bd-121pa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qfho","depends_on_id":"bd-rylze","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:41Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1qls","title":"Add agent naming - give agents memorable names instead of pane numbers (e.g., claude-alpha, codex-bravo)","description":"Add agent naming - give agents memorable names instead of pane numbers.

## Problem Being Solved

Referring to agents by pane number (cc:2, cc:3) is confusing. Names like 'claude-alpha', 'codex-bravo' are more memorable.

## Naming Scheme

### Default Names
- CC agents: claude-alpha, claude-bravo, claude-charlie, ...
- Codex agents: codex-alpha, codex-bravo, ...
- Gemini agents: gemini-alpha, gemini-bravo, ...

### Custom Names
```bash
ntm spawn my_session --cc=2 --names "reviewer,implementer"
```

## Display Integration

### Session List
```
my_session (3 agents)
  claude-alpha (pane 2) - active
  claude-bravo (pane 3) - active  
  codex-charlie (pane 4) - idle
```

### Logs/Watch
```
[claude-alpha] Starting implementation...
[codex-charlie] Reviewing changes...
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-agent-names=SESSION

Returns JSON:
```json
{
  "agents": [
    {"name": "claude-alpha", "pane": 2, "type": "cc"},
    {"name": "claude-bravo", "pane": 3, "type": "cc"}
  ]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Agents get default names based on type (claude-alpha, etc.)
2. --names flag allows custom agent names
3. Names displayed in session list
4. Names used in logs output
5. Robot mode returns name-to-pane mapping
6. Names persist across session lifetime

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/naming/naming_test.go
- Test default name generation
- Test custom name assignment

## E2E Test Script
```bash

+{"id":"bd-1qls","title":"Add agent naming - give agents memorable names instead of pane numbers (e.g., claude-alpha, codex-bravo)","description":"Add agent naming - give agents memorable names instead of pane numbers.

## Problem Being Solved

Referring to agents by pane number (cc:2, cc:3) is confusing. Names like 'claude-alpha', 'codex-bravo' are more memorable.

## Naming Scheme

### Default Names
- CC agents: claude-alpha, claude-bravo, claude-charlie, ...
- Codex agents: codex-alpha, codex-bravo, ...
- Gemini agents: gemini-alpha, gemini-bravo, ...

### Custom Names
```bash
ntm spawn my_session --cc=2 --names "reviewer,implementer"
```

## Display Integration

### Session List
```
my_session (3 agents)
  claude-alpha (pane 2) - active
  claude-bravo (pane 3) - active  
  codex-charlie (pane 4) - idle
```

### Logs/Watch
```
[claude-alpha] Starting implementation...
[codex-charlie] Reviewing changes...
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-agent-names=SESSION

Returns JSON:
```json
{
  "agents": [
    {"name": "claude-alpha", "pane": 2, "type": "cc"},
    {"name": "claude-bravo", "pane": 3, "type": "cc"}
  ]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Agents get default names based on type (claude-alpha, etc.)
2. --names flag allows custom agent names
3. Names displayed in session list
4. Names used in logs output
5. Robot mode returns name-to-pane mapping
6. Names persist across session lifetime

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/naming/naming_test.go
- Test default name generation
- Test custom name assignment

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-AGENT-NAMES] Agent Naming Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-AGENT-NAMES]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing agent naming"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Agents get memorable names instead of pane numbers
2. Names like claude-alpha, codex-bravo generated
3. Names unique within session
4. Names preserved across restarts
5. Robot mode outputs include agent names
6. Unit tests for name generation
7. E2E test verifying names persist","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1qsp","title":"Task: Create CostTracker module","description":"## Purpose
Track estimated API costs across all agents in a session.

## CostTracker Struct
type CostTracker struct {
    mu       sync.Mutex
    sessions map[string]*SessionCost
}

type SessionCost struct {
    Agents    map[string]*AgentCost
    StartTime time.Time
}

type AgentCost struct {
    InputTokens  int
    OutputTokens int
    Model        string
}

## Pricing Table
var modelPricing = map[string]struct{ InputPer1K, OutputPer1K float64 }{
    'claude-opus':   {0.015, 0.075},
    'claude-sonnet': {0.003, 0.015},
    'gpt-4o':        {0.005, 0.015},
    'gemini-pro':    {0.00025, 0.0005},
}

## Key Methods
func (t *CostTracker) RecordPrompt(session, pane string, prompt string)
func (t *CostTracker) RecordResponse(session, pane string, response string)
func (t *CostTracker) GetSessionCost(session string) float64

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tracks input/output tokens per agent
- Calculates cost based on model
- Thread-safe operation
- Persists across session restart

## Technical Notes
Use tiktoken-go for token estimation
Store in .ntm/costs.json","notes":"## Testing Requirements

 {"id":"bd-1qusl","title":"process_triage: Implement rano correlation for improved accuracy","description":"Implement rano correlation for improved process_triage accuracy.

## Purpose

Correlate network activity from rano with process health status to improve accuracy of stuck agent detection.

## Correlation Logic

1. Agent has network activity -> likely not stuck
2. No network for 5+ minutes -> check process state
3. Combine signals for confidence score

## Acceptance Criteria

1. rano data fetched for correlation
2. Network activity correlated with process state
3. Confidence scores improved with rano data
4. Fallback when rano unavailable
5. Unit tests for correlation logic
6.  {"id":"bd-3uydb","title":"Feature: Mode Output Caching","description":"## Summary
Cache mode outputs when context hasn't changed so users can re-run synthesis or compare strategies without re-running expensive analysis.

## Rationale
Caching speeds experimentation and reduces cost when only synthesis strategy changes.

## Implementation
- Cache key includes ContextPack hash + mode_id + mode_version + relevant config hashes (preamble/strategy/model).
- Store serialized ModeOutput in `.ntm/ensemble-cache/` with TTL (default 24h, configurable).
- Invalidate on context change, mode version change, explicit `--no-cache`, or TTL expiry.
- CLI flags: `--use-cache` (default) and `--no-cache`.
- Status output shows hit/miss/invalidations and reason codes.

## Logging
- Log cache hit/miss + invalidation reason (context_hash_changed, ttl_expired, version_bump).

## Tests
- Unit: cache key determinism, TTL expiry, invalidation reasons
- Integration: cache survives restart
- E2E: rerun uses cache; `--no-cache` forces refresh

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cache key deterministically derived
- [ ] Cached outputs restored correctly
- [ ] Invalidations occur for each trigger
- [ ] CLI flags override cache behavior
- [ ] Logs and status show hit/miss metrics

## Dependencies
- Requires ModeOutput schema (bd-1wnxl)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:06:03.033064607Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T03:50:34.593563036Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T03:50:34.593430209Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uydb","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:41Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3uydb","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:14:41Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3v03r","title":"CAAM: Add dashboard widget for account status","description":"Add dashboard widget showing current account and usage per provider.

## Design

### Widget Location
Side panel or bottom section of dashboard.

### Visual Layout
```
┌─ Accounts ────────────────────┐
│ Claude:  acct1@x.com  [███░░] │
│ OpenAI:  acct2@y.com  [████░] │  <- 80% alert (yellow)
│ Gemini:  acct3@z.com  [█░░░░] │
└───────────────────────────────┘
```

### Features
- Progress bar showing usage/limit
- Color coding: green (<60%), yellow (60-80%), red (>80%)
- Click to expand: show all accounts, switch option
- Cooldown indicator for rate-limited accounts

## Implementation

### Model
```go
type AccountsWidget struct {
    caamManager *caam.Manager
    accounts    []AccountStatus
    width       int
    height      int
}

type AccountStatus struct {
    Provider     string
    Email        string
    UsagePercent int
    InCooldown   bool
    CooldownEnd  time.Time
}
```

### View
Use lipgloss for progress bars and colors:
```go
func (w AccountsWidget) View() string {
    // Render each provider row
    // Include progress bar with color
    // Show alert icon if >80%
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/tui/widgets/accounts.go
- Update: internal/tui/dashboard/layout.go

## Refresh
Poll caam status every 30 seconds (or on account switch event).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:53:15.682385815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:39:51.711753399Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:39:51.711702493Z","close_reason":"Implemented accounts.go panel with CAAM status display","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-10qa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:55:42.814227554Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-1vbqd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:55:34.247715173Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-3b52y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:55:47.534632142Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3v03r","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:53:15.779202660Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3v1w7","title":"Visual stall/activity indicators for panes","description":"## Summary
Color-code pane borders to indicate activity status: green (active), yellow (idle), red (stalled).

## Context
- GitHub Issue #18 requests visual indicators for stuck agents
- Helps identify which agents need attention in swarm operations

## Requirements
1. Track last activity timestamp per pane
2. Define thresholds: active (<30s), idle (30s-2min), stalled (>2min)
3. Update tmux pane border colors based on status
4. Configurable thresholds via config file

## Technical Approach
- Poll pane content hash every N seconds to detect changes
- Use tmux pane-border-style and pane-active-border-style
- Colors: green=#00ff00, yellow=#ffff00, red=#ff0000
- Store last_activity_time and last_content_hash per pane

- internal/state/timeline.go - Store activity timestamps
- internal/tui/dashboard/panels/ - Update dashboard display

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pane borders change color based on activity
- [ ] Thresholds configurable in ~/.config/ntm/config.toml
- [ ] Dashboard shows activity status
- [ ] Minimal CPU overhead from polling","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T05:02:35.966466955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T21:42:46.334429478Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3v69","title":"Properly handle tea.WindowSizeMsg for responsive layout","description":"## Problem
Layout doesn't properly adapt when terminal is resized, causing overflow and misalignment.

## Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:1314-1330 - WindowSizeMsg handler
- internal/tui/dashboard/layout.go - Layout calculations
- internal/tui/layout/layout.go - TierForWidth() and layout tiers

## Current Implementation
The WindowSizeMsg handler at line 1314 does:
1. Sets m.width, m.height
2. Sets m.tier based on width
3. Calculates detailWidth for renderer
4. Updates cassSearch size

## What's Missing
1. **Panel size propagation** - Individual panels don't get resized
2. **Content height recalculation** - Available content height not recalculated
3. **Component updates** - Child components (lists, viewports) not resized
4. **Layout mode switching** - Transitions between tiers may be jerky

## Fix Required
\`\`\`go
case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
    m.width = msg.Width
    m.height = msg.Height
    m.tier = layout.TierForWidth(msg.Width)
    
    // Calculate available heights
    headerHeight := 8  // banner + stats + dividers
    footerHeight := 4  // ticker + actions + help
    contentHeight := m.height - headerHeight - footerHeight
    if contentHeight < 10 {
        contentHeight = 10
    }
    m.contentHeight = contentHeight
    
    // Calculate panel widths based on tier
    listWidth, detailWidth := layout.SplitProportions(msg.Width)
    
    // Resize ALL panels
    if m.activityPanel != nil {
        m.activityPanel.SetSize(listWidth-4, contentHeight-2)
    }
    if m.beadsPanel != nil {
        m.beadsPanel.SetSize(listWidth-4, contentHeight-2)
    }
    if m.alertsPanel != nil {
        m.alertsPanel.SetSize(listWidth-4, contentHeight-2)
    }
    if m.detailPanel != nil {
        m.detailPanel.SetSize(detailWidth-4, contentHeight-2)
    }
    if m.tickerPanel != nil {
        m.tickerPanel.SetSize(m.width-4, 1)
    }
    
    // Update renderer for markdown
    m.initRenderer(detailWidth - 4)
    
    // Update CASS search modal
    m.cassSearch.SetSize(int(float64(msg.Width)*0.6), int(float64(msg.Height)*0.6))
    
    return m, tea.Batch(cmds...)
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Resizing terminal updates all panel dimensions
- [ ] Content stays within bounds at all sizes
- [ ] Smooth transitions between layout tiers
- [ ] Minimum terminal size (80x24) handled gracefully
- [ ] Very large terminals (300x100) don't break layout

## Unit Tests
1. TestWindowSizeMsg_UpdatesAllDimensions - all size fields updated
2. TestWindowSizeMsg_PanelsResized - each panel's SetSize called
3. TestWindowSizeMsg_MinimumSize - graceful handling of 80x24
4. TestWindowSizeMsg_TierTransition - tier changes correctly on width thresholds
5. TestWindowSizeMsg_ContentHeightCalculation - height math is correct

## E2E Tests
1. VHS tape: start at 80x24, resize to 200x60, resize to 100x30
2. Verify no content overflow at any size
3. Verify tier transitions are smooth

## Logging
- DEBUG: Log all dimension calculations on resize
- DEBUG: Log panel sizes after resize
- INFO: Log tier transitions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:12:16.202239127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:03:47.217652348Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:03:43.919593873Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: WindowSizeMsg handler properly resizes all panels, tier transitions work, all 5 acceptance criteria tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v69","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:17:25.829321010Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-3v69","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 3 missing unit tests: TestWindowSizeMsg_MinimumSize (graceful handling of 80x24 and extreme sizes), TestWindowSizeMsg_TierTransition (all 5 tier thresholds with 10 subtests), TestWindowSizeMsg_ContentHeightCalculation (7 height scenarios including clamping). Also fixed pre-existing vet error in dashboard_layout_test.go (invalid type assertion). All dashboard tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-19T09:03:47Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3vc3s","title":"Implement structured error responses","description":"Implement structured error responses for all failure modes.

## Why

All errors must include enough info to diagnose without manual inspection.
Consistent error format enables automated recovery decisions.

## Error Response Schema

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "SOFT_EXIT_FAILED",
    "message": "Agent did not respond to Ctrl-C within 3s",
    "phase": "soft_exit",
    "pane": 4,
    "details": {
      "child_pid": 12345,
      "process_state": "running",
      "last_output": "... truncated ...",
      "attempted_actions": ["ctrl-c-1", "ctrl-c-2", "wait-3s"]
    },
    "recovery_hint": "Try --robot-restart-pane with automatic kill -9 fallback"
  }
}
```

## Error Codes

| Code | Phase | Meaning |
|------|-------|---------|
| PANE_NOT_FOUND | init | Pane doesn't exist |
| SOFT_EXIT_FAILED | soft_exit | Ctrl-C didn't work |
| HARD_KILL_FAILED | hard_kill | kill -9 didn't work |
| SHELL_NOT_RETURNED | post_exit | No shell prompt after exit |
| CC_LAUNCH_FAILED | launch | cc command failed |
| CC_INIT_TIMEOUT | launch | cc didn't initialize |
| BEAD_NOT_FOUND | prompt | Bead ID doesn't exist |
| PROMPT_SEND_FAILED | prompt | Failed to send prompt |

## Required Information per Error

Each error must include:
- error.code: Machine-parseable error type
- error.message: Human-readable description
- error.phase: Which step failed
- error.details: Context for debugging
- error.recovery_hint: Suggested next action

## Logging

Log all errors in structured format for post-mortem analysis:
```json
{"level": "error", "code": "SOFT_EXIT_FAILED", "pane": 4, "timestamp": "..."}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:38:27.569255121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:17:45.172053798Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:17:45.171952998Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vc3s","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:38:27.597236579Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -1519,6 +1519,7 @@ type Agent struct {
 	Type     string `json:"type"`              // claude, codex, gemini
 	Variant  string `json:"variant,omitempty"` // Model alias or persona name
 	Pane     string `json:"pane"`
+	Name     string `json:"name,omitempty"`     // Memorable agent name (e.g., claude-alpha)
 	Window   int    `json:"window"`
 	PaneIdx  int    `json:"pane_idx"`
 	IsActive bool   `json:"is_active"`

@@ -0,0 +1,53 @@
+// Package tmux provides a wrapper around tmux commands.
+// pane_border.go implements per-pane border style control for activity indicators.
+package tmux
+
+import (
+	"context"
+	"fmt"
+)
+
+// SetPaneBorderStyle sets the border style for a specific pane using
+// select-pane -t <target> -P 'pane-border-style=fg=<color>'.
+// The color should be a tmux color name or hex value (e.g., "#00ff00").
+func (c *Client) SetPaneBorderStyle(target, color string) error {
+	return c.SetPaneBorderStyleContext(context.Background(), target, color)
+}
+
+// SetPaneBorderStyleContext sets the pane border style with context/cancellation support.
+func (c *Client) SetPaneBorderStyleContext(ctx context.Context, target, color string) error {
+	style := fmt.Sprintf("fg=%s", color)
+	return c.RunSilentContext(ctx, "select-pane", "-t", target, "-P",
+		fmt.Sprintf("pane-border-style=%s", style))
+}
+
+// SetPaneBorderStyle sets the border style for a pane (default client).
+func SetPaneBorderStyle(target, color string) error {
+	return DefaultClient.SetPaneBorderStyle(target, color)
+}
+
+// SetPaneBorderStyleContext sets the border style with context support (default client).
+func SetPaneBorderStyleContext(ctx context.Context, target, color string) error {
+	return DefaultClient.SetPaneBorderStyleContext(ctx, target, color)
+}
+
+// ResetPaneBorderStyle resets a pane's border style to the default.
+func (c *Client) ResetPaneBorderStyle(target string) error {
+	return c.ResetPaneBorderStyleContext(context.Background(), target)
+}
+
+// ResetPaneBorderStyleContext resets border style with context support.
+func (c *Client) ResetPaneBorderStyleContext(ctx context.Context, target string) error {
+	return c.RunSilentContext(ctx, "select-pane", "-t", target, "-P",
+		"pane-border-style=default")
+}
+
+// ResetPaneBorderStyle resets a pane's border style (default client).
+func ResetPaneBorderStyle(target string) error {
+	return DefaultClient.ResetPaneBorderStyle(target)
+}
+
+// ResetPaneBorderStyleContext resets border style with context support (default client).
+func ResetPaneBorderStyleContext(ctx context.Context, target string) error {
+	return DefaultClient.ResetPaneBorderStyleContext(ctx, target)
+}
